title: Fixing Incorrect Node Height in a Binary Tree

statement.markdown: Given the root of a binary tree, calculate the height of the tree. The height of a binary tree is the number of nodes along the longest path from the root node down to the furthest leaf node. Your solution should traverse the tree in a way that ensures a correct height calculation. However, the starter code has a bug that can cause incorrect results. Fix the bug to ensure the height is calculated correctly.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a binary tree where each node contains an integer value and two child pointers.
statement.outputFormat: The height of the binary tree.

examples.input:
Input: [3,9,20,null,null,15,7]
Output: 3
Explanation: The height of binary tree is 3.

Input: [1,null,2]
Output: 2
Explanation: The height of binary tree is 2.

hints.text: Check that the tree has a root node.

editorial.title: Editorial
commonMistakes: Incorrectly calculating the height of the tree
